findings.title: Toggle time-display button lacks explicit current-state announcement
findings.why_it_matters: aria-label is the static string 'Toggle time display'. Screen-reader users don't hear which mode is active until they activate it. aria-pressed conveys toggle state but combining it with a descriptive label like 'Show remaining time' / 'Show total duration' improves clarity (WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value).
findings.suggested_fix: Make aria-label dynamic: aria-label={showRemainingTime ? 'Show total duration' : 'Show remaining time'} so SR users hear the action that will happen on activation.

findings.title: Skip-interval changes invalidate pause/resume callback identity unnecessarily
findings.why_it_matters: handleSkipBack/handleSkipForward useCallback deps include both the underlying skip function AND the seconds value. When the user changes the interval in settings, MediaSession re-registers handlers and useKeyboardShortcuts likely re-binds. This is correct (intentional per the comment), but worth noting if MediaSession re-registration causes audible glitches on some browsers — verified safe per Unit 0 discovery comment.
evidence: Comment explicitly addresses this: 'verified via Unit 0 discovery'
findings.suggested_fix: No change required; the comment justifies the design. Consider adding a brief regression test that toggling skip interval mid-playback does not introduce an audible gap.

findings.title: VALID_TIMERS Set in settings.ts hydration duplicates store's allow-list
findings.why_it_matters: Hardcoded ['off', 5, 10, 15, 30, 45, 60, 'end-of-chapter'] in hydration mirrors VALID_TIMERS in useAudiobookPrefsStore.ts. If a future preset is added to the store but not here, Supabase->local hydration will silently drop the value. Same pre-existing pattern existed before this PR (without 5/10), so this change preserves rather than introduces the duplication, but is now larger.
findings.suggested_fix: Export VALID_TIMERS (or a guard isSleepTimerDefault) from useAudiobookPrefsStore and import it in settings.ts hydration to keep allow-lists in sync. Apply the same pattern for the new VALID_SKIP_BACK/FORWARD when those gain hydration validation.

findings.title: Hydration accepts unvalidated skipBackSeconds/skipForwardSeconds from Supabase
findings.why_it_matters: hydrateSettingsFromSupabase calls setSkipBackSeconds/setSkipForwardSeconds with any number from Supabase. The store setter validates against VALID_SKIP_BACK_SET / VALID_SKIP_FORWARD_SET and falls back to the default, which is safe today. However, this couples external trust to internal validation: if a future refactor moves validation out of the setter, hydration becomes the attack surface. Aligning hydration with the explicit allow-list (as already done for defaultSleepTimer) hardens the contract.
evidence: settings.ts:392 defaultSleepTimer is gated by validTimers.has(...) before being applied — inconsistent treatment
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the defaultSleepTimer pattern: gate skipBackSeconds/skipForwardSeconds with VALID_SKIP_BACK / VALID_SKIP_FORWARD allow-lists in hydrateSettingsFromSupabase before calling the setter.

findings.title: Loose corruption-handling test relies on setter fallback rather than load-time validation
findings.why_it_matters: The 'falls through to defaults when persisted skip values are corrupted' test reloads the module and asserts defaults. This passes because loadPersistedPrefs validates with VALID_SKIP_*_SET. Good. But the assertion expects skipBackSeconds=15 / skipForwardSeconds=30 — these match defaults, so a regression that silently disables hydration of these fields would also pass. Consider asserting that a *valid* persisted value is restored in the same test file path (already done in the previous test) — coverage is fine, just noting the asymmetric assertion strength.
evidence: useAudiobookPrefsStore.test.ts:309-318 corruption test asserts only the default values
findings.suggested_fix: Optional: add a single assertion that valid persisted values survive a reload to the corruption test as a paired sanity check, or accept the current split coverage.

residual_risks: Skip-interval handler identity changes when settings change. Verified safe per Unit 0 discovery, but media session re-register on mid-playback change is untested in CI.
Cross-device Supabase hydration of skipBackSeconds/skipForwardSeconds is not allow-list gated (see F4).
testing_gaps: No E2E covering: change skip interval -> press keyboard arrow -> verify new offset applied (deferred per plan).
No test for AudioMiniPlayer reading new skip seconds (component test missing; plan deferred to manual smoke).

coverage.lint: 1 pre-existing warning (component-size/max-lines on AudiobookRenderer.tsx — pre-existing, file already over threshold before this PR)
